summary: Should the MCP server tarball SHA be signed inside the DSSE attestation predicate at release time, or revert to in-tree bundle with tighter freshness gate?

options.description: Sign npm tarball sha512 inside DSSE predicate at release time

summary: Ship npm-shrinkwrap.json alongside plugin.json to enforce integrity hashes on first-install of mcp-server + transitive deps?

options.description: Ship npm-shrinkwrap.json regenerated on each plugin version bump

rationale: Operator decision 2026-05-26 via decision-rubric walkthrough. Picked over (B) npm-native provenance to keep ONE trust anchor (operator's trusted-reviewers.yaml pubkey set, already used for v6 review attestations) rather than introducing GitHub OIDC as a second anchor. Picked over (C) revert-to-in-tree because AISDLC-385 just eliminated that friction. Picked over (D) status-quo because npm registry compromise IS in the threat model. Reuses existing AI-SDLC v6 Merkle envelope infrastructure; composes with DEC-0002 shrinkwrap layer for defense-in-depth.

Operator decision 2026-05-26 via decision-rubric walkthrough. Picked over (B) npm-audit-signatures because registry signature coverage is uneven across transitive deps. Picked over (C) pin-direct-only because partial mitigation is worst-of-both. Picked over (D) status-quo because no install-time integrity guarantee for any dep is unacceptable for MCP server's blast radius (operator credentials + repo write access). Patch-velocity cost mitigated by existing Dependabot/Renovate auto-PR flow that drives transitive bumps. Composes with DEC-0001 signed-tarball layer.

summary: identityclass-classification-disagreement: did-compiler.ts ic() defaults to 'evolving'; canonical RFC-0028 §7.1 says 'core'

options.description: Align did-compiler.ts ic() default to canonical 'core'
Carve documented cross-layer exemption keeping 'evolving' as DID-scoring default
Revise canonical RFC-0028 §7.1 taxonomy to per-domain defaults

summary: PR #798 (AISDLC-454) attestation invalid: committed v5 envelope contentHashV4-mismatch vs CI verifier; v6 path needs transcript leaves absent in ad-hoc tmux flow

options.description: Option 3 — check if the mismatch is a verifier/CHURN-list issue and the v5 envelope is legitimately valid (preferred: diagnose before re-signing)
Option 2 — re-sign v5 recomputing contentHashV4 against the CHURN-excluded file set (sanctioned ad-hoc path per CLAUDE.md)
Do NOT take Option 1 (re-sign v6 with SYNTHETIC leaves) — synthetic leaves = fabricated review evidence, violates no-fabrication rule

summary: Drain tail (#798/#804/#805) blocked by v6 attestation head-binding re-sign churn; AISDLC-475 is the root-cause fix

options.description: Prioritize AISDLC-475 walkthrough (remove per-SHA bridge, fix signer/verifier head-binding asymmetry) — ends the re-sign loop so armed PRs merge
Operator admin-merges the 3 armed+reviewed PRs bypassing the stale attestation commit-status
Leave PRs armed; they merge once 475 lands (work not wasted, but stalls until operator returns)

rationale: Walkthrough 2026-05-31 (operator Dominique + engineering). CHOSEN: Fix B now (remove per-SHA bridge — signer writes patch-id envelope only; pre-push hook idempotency keys off patch-id only; verifier keeps per-SHA lookup READ-ONLY for legacy 1-release soak; exclusion-list lockstep AC#6; AC#7 hermetic tests; AC#8 replay-protection preserved). TARGET END-STATE: B+ (also cut the post-sign chore-commit so HEAD never moves after signing) filed as fast-follow. Corrected root cause: NOT auto-rearm [skip ci] commits (auto-rearm adds none) — it is the bash hook/verifier head-binding ASYMMETRY. Dispatching AISDLC-475 (Fix B).

DEDUP -> subsumed by DEC-0008. Investigation complete: #798's mismatch is the v6 signer/verifier head-binding asymmetry (re-sign loop), NOT a v5 churn-list bug. Structural fix tracked + resolved under DEC-0008 / AISDLC-475. #798 merges once 475 lands or via operator admin-merge.

summary: execute-parallel spawned-session permission model (AISDLC-485): adopt --dangerously-skip-permissions opt-in

options.description: Option (a) — spawned claude gets --dangerously-skip-permissions, GATED behind the existing execute-parallel confirmation step (operator explicitly acknowledges reduced prompting before windows open); never silently defaulted. Chosen: operator authorized autonomous parallel dispatch + 12h full-autonomy drain 2026-06-01.
Option (b) — pre-seed per-worktree permission allowlist (more surgical, more setup)
Option (c) — env-var auto-approve (feasibility unconfirmed)

rationale: Operator authorized autonomous parallel dispatch + 12h full-autonomy drain (2026-06-01). Option (a): --dangerously-skip-permissions GATED behind the execute-parallel confirmation step, operator-acknowledged, never silently defaulted (AC#2). This is the recommended path per the task body; the autonomy intent makes it the operator's standing decision.

summary: AISDLC-487: main branch-protection posture — strict-up-to-date + no merge-queue causes merge starvation under parallel/autonomous dispatch

options.description: Re-introduce a GitHub merge queue — PRs serialize + auto-rebase, no up-to-date race. Cost: queue latency. (Was dropped in AISDLC-400.)
Set strict=false (drop require-up-to-date) — PRs merge without rebasing onto every intervening landing. Cost: cross-PR merge-skew risk (the AISDLC-406 main-health-monitor backstops it).
Keep strict=true but add robust auto-rebase+re-sign automation so the race self-heals without operator intervention. Cost: still re-runs full CI per rebase.

rationale: Root cause is require-up-to-date (strict=true), not the absent queue: strict=true forces a rebase of every other open PR on each landing (O(N^2) rebase+CI+re-sign). merge-queue serializes (opposite of concurrent disjoint merges) + full CI per entry; auto-rebase is the status quo that re-staled #828. strict-false composes with AISDLC-491 (patch-scoped verifier = attestation rebase-invariant for disjoint PRs): #491 removes attestation invalidation, strict-false removes the forced rebase = concurrent disjoint merges. Merge-skew backstopped by AISDLC-406 main-health-monitor (full suite per push, P0 issue). Caveat: [main-health] issues become load-bearing P0.

summary: Relax v6 attestation subject.sha1 head-binding when patch-id + Merkle root + trusted signature already verify (eliminate orphan-subject CI failures)

options.description: Drop the subject.sha1 reachability requirement when content patch-id matches the envelope filename AND the Merkle root verifies AND the signature is from a trusted key — content is already bound by patch-id (in-toto/SLSA-style). Eliminates the orphan-after-rebase CI-vs-local asymmetry.

Keep subject.sha1 head-binding as-is; rely on the tactical pre-push guard (re-sign reachable subject) to prevent orphan subjects. Lower change surface, retains belt-and-suspenders commit binding, but the local-passes/CI-fails fragility remains latent.
Keep binding but have CI deep-fetch/unshallow so the subject is reachable. Rejected on examination — orphaned (rebased-away) commits are unreferenced and not fetchable by SHA.

body: Root cause: PR #912 (AISDLC-538) failed the CI attestation gate because subject.sha1 pointed at an orphaned pre-rebase commit unreachable in CI's shallow clone; the AISDLC-448 tree-equivalence relaxation works locally (orphan in object store) but not CI -> local-passes/CI-fails. The patch-id (envelope filename) already content-binds the envelope to the exact diff, so subject.sha1 is largely redundant with patch-id+Merkle+signature. Security note: an envelope signed for diff-X's patch-id cannot validate diff-Y (different patch-id -> different filename -> not found); cross-PR reuse requires byte-identical diffs (benign). Tracked alongside the tactical guard task. Routed to operator (Engineering/Security) since it changes the attestation trust model.

rationale: Operator decision 2026-06-13: relax. patch-id (envelope filename, from diff content) already content-binds the envelope; Merkle root + trusted-key signature authenticate reviewer evidence; subject.sha1 commit-binding is redundant and the sole source of the orphan-after-rebase CI fragility (4 recovery re-signs in the 2026-06-12 drain, incl PR #912). In-toto/SLSA/Sigstore bind to content digests not commit SHAs. Replay counter-argument answered: cross-PR reuse needs a byte-identical diff = same patch-id = same review (benign). Impl: verifier accepts when content-patch-id==filename + Merkle verifies + trusted signature, WITHOUT subject.sha1 reachability. AISDLC-545 becomes belt-and-suspenders; fold relaxation into AISDLC-543.
